title: Psacalium sinuatum
taxonomy: Psacalium sinuatum belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is part of the tribe Millerieae. It is classified within the genus Psacalium. Psacalium sinuatum is native to Mexico and is a type of flowering plant. The classification of Psacalium sinuatum is consistent with the classification of other plants within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Psacalium sinuatum, also known as tree marigold, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Psacalium sinuatum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with tree marigold include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from digestive issues to skin irritations.
